title: On limit points of the sequence of normalized prime gaps

thm:1.1.summary: For any 9-tuple of nonneg $\beta_1 \le \cdots \le \beta_9$, at least one $\beta_j - \beta_i$ lies in $L = $ limit points of $\{d_n / \log p_n\}$. Quantitative density of the limit set.

cor:1.2.summary: Lebesgue measure: $\lambda([0,T] \cap L) \ge T/22$ unconditional, $\ge (1-o(1)) T/8$ asymptotic. Shows $L$ has positive density on the half-line.

thm:1.3.summary: $m$-tuple version: for fixed $m \ge 2$, the $m$-tuple limit set $L_m$ hits $S_m(\boldsymbol{\beta})$ for any $\boldsymbol{\beta} \in \mathbb{R}^k$ with $k \ge k_0(m)$. Multivariate generalization of thm:1.1.

lem:4.1.summary: Refined Page-Landau zero-free region: among primitive $\chi \bmod q$ with $q \le T$ and $P^+(q) \le P$, at most one $L(s,\chi)$ has a zero in $\mathrm{Re}(s) > 1 - c/\log P$, $|\mathrm{Im}(s)| \le \exp(\log P / \sqrt{\log T})$; if it exists, $\chi$ is real and unique.

thm:4.2.summary: Modified Bombieri-Vinogradov theorem: BV-style equidistribution for primes restricted to moduli $q$ with $P^+(q)$ bounded. The level-of-distribution input feeding Maynard's sieve in this setting.

edges.note: Modified BV is the level-of-distribution Maynard's sieve consumes.
Zero-free region for primes to bounded-$P^+(q)$ moduli.
Lebesgue-measure consequence of the 9-tuple density statement.

main_results.description: thm:1.1 (9-tuple density of $L$), cor:1.2 (Lebesgue measure $\ge T/22$), thm:1.3 ($m$-tuple version).

analytic_inputs.description: lem:4.1 (refined Page-Landau zero-free region) + thm:4.2 (modified Bombieri-Vinogradov for primes-at-smooth-moduli). The level-of-distribution apparatus, sharper than standard BV, that powers Maynard's multidim sieve in this application.

Maynard:smallgaps.title: Small gaps between primes
Maynard:smallgaps.role: method_source — the multidim sieve consumed by thm:1.1

Hildebrand:1985.title: On the number of positive integers $\le x$ and free of prime factors $> y$
Hildebrand:1985.role: foundational — smooth-number distribution, used in lem:4.1
